read.image: Sin City lit and no one in it
read.lens: freedom that turns out to be absence
read.take: Las Vegas was supposed to be freedom. No one to answer to, no one around to judge, the whole city burning at 4 a.m. That turns out to be the problem. He is going through withdrawals — from her, from the high, from anything that makes him feel real — and the lights of Sin City are filling in for the touch he cannot sleep without. He drives into the sunrise to find her, blinded by everything he chose instead.
read.contradiction: Sin City gave him everything he asked for and none of what he needed.

arc.label: Wanting
arc.scene: He has been trying to call. He has been on his own for long enough. The city is cold and empty, and freedom with no one to answer to turns out to feel exactly like being alone. He cannot see clearly when she is gone.

arc.label: The Need
arc.scene: Blinded by the lights. Drowning in the night. He cannot sleep until he feels a touch and the city cannot give him that.

arc.label: Still Driving
arc.scene: Coming back to tell her what he could never say on the phone, certain he will not let her go this time. He drives toward her through everything still blinding him.

lines.line: I can't sleep until I feel your touch
I said, ooh, I'm blinded by the lights

Sin City's cold and empty
No one's around to judge me

I'm just coming back to let you know
I could never say it on the phone

read.texture: Glossy retro synthwave, bright and propulsive, all chrome and cold night air.
